_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Following surgical debridement of acute or chronic wounds (e.g., traumatic wounds, necrotizing infection, pressure ulcer)
Reconstructive surgery (e.g., chronic non healing burn wound, skin graft)
To prevent surgical wound infection (ie, prophylactic) and as a means of instillation therapy

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, and advice for admission

ppd_preauth.q: Are detailed Clinical notes (history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment) available?
Did clinical presentation confirm the diagnosis?
Is the indication for procedure documented?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and line of treatment submitted?
Are the detailed Procedure Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge provided?

common_queries: Clinical presentation not indicative of Negative Pressure Wound Therapy or diagnosis not confirmed at pre-authorization.
Pre-authorization clinical notes lack sufficient detail regarding history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, or planned line of treatment.
Documentation of the indication for Negative Pressure Wound Therapy is missing or unclear at pre-authorization.
Mandatory clinical photographs (pre-operative or post-operative) are missing, unclear, or not relevant.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) for claim submission are missing or do not contain daily vitals and line of treatment.
Detailed Procedure/Operative notes are missing or incomplete at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Negative pressure wound therapy (NPWT), refers to wound dressing systems that continuously or intermittently apply sub-atmospheric pressure to the surface of a wound.
NPWT is an adjunctive therapy for the management of mainly chronic and difficult wounds and some acute wounds.
The wound care system consists of an open-cell foam dressing, semi-occlusive adhesive cover, fluid collection system, and suction pump.
contraindications: Exposed vital structures – NPWT, in the presence of exposed organs, blood vessels, or vascular grafts, increases the risk for tissue erosion, which can lead to enteric fistula or hemorrhage
Ongoing infection or devitalized tissue (relative contraindication)
